prompt.content:
Can you determine the FY 26 benefit-cost ratio of filling the vacancies by Service Line based on marginal revenue over marginal cost?

Assume there is a 5% inflation increase from FY 25 to FY 26 for price per unit volume and personnel related costs. Also, the cohort size of the vacancy will impact onboarding/ training efficiency and hence unit productivity FY 26 as follows:
- More than 20 vacancies: 70% efficiency
- 10 to 20 vacancies (inclusive): 80% efficiency
- Fewer than 10 vacancies: 90% efficiency

Refer to the following files in the filesystem for the corresponding data:
•	Lakeshore_Financial_Statements_FY2024-2025 Updated.xlsx – Use the departmental P&L as the source of truth for the financial data
•	Service_Line_Volumes.xlsx – Use the volume summary as the source of truth for volume data
•	Cost_to_Fill_By_Service_Line.csv - Only include service lines that have cost to fill data.

Please state in the console the service line with the highest FY 26 benefit-cost ratio and the value. State the final answer in two decimal places and do not round intermediate calculations. Please provide a full report of your analysis in the console.

rubrics.name: States that the highest FY 26 benefit-cost ratio is 3.27 (acceptable range... (ver_bcfaa61a941c4032be2e52b9b3c29d16)

messages.content: States that the highest FY 26 benefit-cost ratio is 3.27 (acceptable range 3.24 to 3.30).

rubrics.name: States that the service line with the highest FY 26 benefit-cost ratio is... (ver_f39f246e3bb04039a8a65a4828162f6e)

messages.content: States that the service line with the highest FY 26 benefit-cost ratio is Women and Infants.

golden_responses.golden_text_response: The service line with the highest FY 26 benefit-cost ratio is Women and Infants. The value is 3.27.
